title: The Lando Calrissian Adventures (omnibus)
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |not_appearance=1 |image=[[File:Adventures_of_lando.jpg]] |title=''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'' |author=[[L. Neil Smith]] |cover artist=[[William Schmidt]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Del Rey]] |early release= |release date=[[June]] [[1994]]{{RandomHouseOld|url=delrey/starwars/catalog/display.pperl?isbn=0345391101|text=The Adventures of Lando Calrissian}} |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=409 |isbn=0345391101 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline= |series=''[[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures]]'' |preceded by=''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' |followed by=[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]] }} '''''The Lando Calrissian Adventures''''' is a compilation of the [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|series of the same name]] by [[L. Neil Smith]]. It details the experiences of [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] as captain of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] before he lost it to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Lando is accompanied by his droid co-pilot, [[Vuffi Raa]], and opposed by [[Rokur Gepta]] (part of a mystic order called the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]]). This trilogy contains the books ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' and ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''. ==Continuity== This trilogy has a number of aspects peculiar to it. Many of them were later [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] to fit better into the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] (EU). Notable among them are: *Lando Calrissian's noteworthy achievements of saving or at least befriending no less than three highly advanced civilizations. Two of them ([[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] and [[Oswaft]]) were never mentioned again in EU except several [[roleplaying game]] supplements and resource books. However, the third civilization—the [[Silentium]]—appeared in brief excerpts from the [[The Han Solo Trilogy (hardcover)|Han Solo Trilogy]] when the droid character Vuffi Raa appeared and was mentioned as joining his impressively advanced comrades. The Silentium were also much later retconned to be one of the two warring factions provoking [[Yuuzhan Vong]] to hate technology. *Lando Calrissian's [[Stingbeam/Legends|stingbeam]]-type blaster pistol was designed for the trilogy and never mentioned again. *The weapons on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] starships in the third book are mentioned nowhere else in EU. *The ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} arsenal has different weapon placement from the movies and other books. However, the second tome of the Han Solo Trilogy, ''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'', reveals that [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]] were originally installed in a peculiar way and Han Solo, after winning the ''Falcon'', changed their placement for both better range and greater cover area of fire. *The cards and game mechanics of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] were first introduced in these books. The full game and rules were later presented in [[West End Games]]' ''[[Crisis on Cloud City (adventure book)|Crisis on Cloud City]]'' adventure, loosely based on the game described in the Lando books but with some variations (such as dropping the fact that fives are wild, a nonsensical rule anyway in a game where the total sum of a player's cards determines victory). Most subsequent sabacc appearances in the EU followed the West End Games rules. It was later stated that the game played throughout this trilogy is a variant called [[Centran sabacc/Legends|Centran sabacc]]. *Names of most of the mentioned planets had numbers, unlike most of planets mentioned elsewhere. However, all of the planets mentioned are located in a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] region called [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. This could mean that Centrality used its own naming convention. The trilogy was meant to be augmented with the ''[[Lando Calrissian Adventures Sourcebook]]'' by West End Games, but this project was canceled. While part of the Legends continuity, the trilogy was humorously referenced in [[canon]] in the [[2018]] film ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story]]'' as "[[Calrissian Chronicles|The Calrissian Chronicles]]," Lando's self-narrated memoirs. The two first volumes of the Polish edition of the trilogy had the front and back covers designed independently from the original cover art. However, the cover designs of these books had little in common with the stories therein: ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' had pictures of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] next to Lando, while on ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]''{{'s}} cover several [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guardsmen]] surrounded Calrissian with a [[Tibanna gas platform]] and some [[Thranta/Legends|thrantas]] in the foreground. ==Media== ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|0-345-39110-1}}; June 1994, [[Del Rey]], 416-page mass market paperback *{{ISBN|0-345-39443-7}}; September 28, 1994, [[Del Rey]], 409-page trade paperback *{{ISBN|9780593726099}}; [[June 4]], [[2024]]; [[Random House Worlds]]; Trade Paperback{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/169281/the-lando-calrissian-adventures-star-wars-legends-by-l-neil-smith|text=The Adventures of Lando Calrissian: Star Wars Legends}} ===Cover gallery=== File:The_Lando_Calrissian_Adventures_1994.jpg|[[1994]] mass market paperback File:Adventures_of_lando.jpg|[[1994]] trade paperback File:The_Lando_Calrissian_Adventures_2005.jpg|[[2005]] mass market paperback File:LandoCalrissianAdventures-Legends.png|Legends File:LandoCalrissianAdventures_De.jpg|German - ''Lando Calrissian - Rebell des Sonnensystems'' File:Lando_Calrissian_Adventures_Essential_Legends_Cover.jpg|''[[The Essential Legends Collection]]'' cover ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|150|Launchpad|Launch Pad}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Trilogy-box |title=''[[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures]]''
